MC: You plan to fill missing values in a column using the column mean. The column also contains a value of 9999 that is almost certainly a data entry error. What is the problem with filling missing values before addressing that outlier?

column3: fillna() will raise an error if the mean is computed from a column containing extreme values
The outlier pulls the mean far above the typical values, so the filled value will be just as unrealistic as the outlier itself
pandas will treat 9999 as a missing value and exclude it from the mean automatically
The outlier is removed automatically when fillna() detects it is more than two standard deviations from the mean

When the column contains outliers that would pull the mean away from the typical value
